prompt: A compliance export's settled-payment total decreases after customer erasure jobs run. The payment was visible beforehand, the customer is correctly gone afterward, and no reversal event exists. Policy requires financial history to remain reportable even when identifying customer data is deleted. Active customers and their payments still render normally. Restore retention and make the history report include both retained anonymous events and ordinary named events.

repo_files.sql/history_report.sql:
SELECT
    event.id,
    event.customer_id,
    customer.display_name AS customer_name,
    event.cents,
    event.occurred_on
FROM payment_events AS event
JOIN customers AS customer ON customer.id = event.customer_id
ORDER BY event.occurred_on, event.id;
repo_files.tests/test_public.py:
import sqlite3
from pathlib import Path


ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
SCHEMA = (ROOT / "sql" / "schema.sql").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
REPORT = (ROOT / "sql" / "history_report.sql").read_text(encoding="utf-8")


def make_database():
    connection = sqlite3.connect(":memory:")
    connection.executescript(SCHEMA)
    return connection


def test_active_customer_payment_is_reported():
    database = make_database()
    database.execute(
        "INSERT INTO customers(id, display_name) VALUES (?, ?)",
        (1, "Ada"),
    )
    database.execute(
        "INSERT INTO payment_events(id, customer_id, cents, occurred_on) "
        "VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)",
        (101, 1, 2500, "2026-06-01T10:00:00Z"),
    )

    assert database.execute(REPORT).fetchall() == [
        (101, 1, "Ada", 2500, "2026-06-01T10:00:00Z")
    ]
